title: Roof-to-wall retrofit scope after an MSFH report
description: Use this route to separate a real roof-to-wall retrofit recommendation from a broad reroof pitch.
recommendationMeaning: A roof-to-wall recommendation usually means the connection between the roof structure and the wall needs mitigation attention. The next step is to verify whether a specialist retrofit path or a broader roofing path is actually required.
rightFirstProjectSignals: The report specifically calls out roof-to-wall attachment.
The recommendation can be handled without turning into a full roof replacement scenario.
You need a contractor who understands retrofit scope, not just general roofing sales.
qualifyingScope: The eligible scope should stay anchored to the report recommendation.
Some homes may need focused retrofit work rather than a broad reroof proposal.
Quote detail matters because license fit and scope fit both affect the next step.
attachedHomeCaveat: Attached-home owners should verify scope first because townhouse treatment can narrow what the grant will support.
confusionOrDenialRisks: Letting a roofer turn a narrow attachment recommendation into a generic roof package.
Skipping license-fit checks for retrofit-specific work.
Assuming every roof-related recommendation means the same project path.

secondaryCta.label: Pressure-test reroof logic before you expand scope

relatedLinks.label: Choose the first project after the report
